---
title: "Cauchy & Misc"
description: |
  Yes, welcome, this is a whole page of my cat and links to miscellaneous things I like.
output:
  distill::distill_article:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

# Cauchy

![Cauchy's response to "Foods?"](cauchy2020.jpg)

This is Cauchy, named for [this guy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Augustin-Louis_Cauchy). I adopted him as a kitten from a shelter on my birthday several years ago. Words Cauchy currently responds to are his name (and also variants "Cauch" and "Mr. Cauch"), "foods", "treats", and "drugs" (catnip). 

![Sometimes he gets haircuts!](cauchylion.jpg)

![Sometimes he plays games!](cauchbugs.gif)

![Usually he interrupts work.](nickcauch.jpg)




# Links

I like these pieces that introduce some problems in academic science and propose possible solutions.

### Organization of academic science:
  + The creation and waiting room-ness of the modern postdoc position: [Can Twitter Save Science? by Alex Danco](https://alexdanco.com/2020/02/15/can-twitter-save-science/) 
  + The one PI - one lab model: [Academic precarity and the single PI model by Romain Brette](http://romainbrette.fr/academic-precarity-and-the-single-pi-lab-model/)

  + Scientists aren't trained to mentor. That's a problem. By [Adam Ruben for Science](https://www.sciencemag.org/careers/2020/08/scientists-aren-t-trained-mentor-s-problem)
  + Ben Barres proposed a mentoring index for PIs [in his 2013 piece on choosing a graduate advisor](barres-2013.pdf). Ben argued for assessing trainee h-indices, but well-mentored trainees aspire to careers both within and outside of academia.    

### Stats misunderstanding and misuse is widespread in the biological sciences:
  + An accessible introduction to the problem by [Tom Siegfried for Nautilus](http://nautil.us/issue/4/the-unlikely/sciences-significant-stats-problem)
  + Especially prevalent in neuroscience: 
    + [Nieuwenhuis et al., 2011](https://doi.org/10.1038/nn.2886)
    + [Button et al., 2013](https://doi.org/10.1038/nrn3475)
